6252	    lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway, lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule,
6253	    def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
6254	  % NOTE (prover target): this is the residual `htilde` discharged by the new public theorem
6255	  % `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`, built directly in
6256	  % CechAcyclic.lean (where the polymorphic `SectionCechModule`/`SectionCechTilde` core is in scope)
6257	  % and consumed by the reduced forms `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` /
6258	  % `affine_serre_vanishing_of_tildeVanishing`.
6259	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
6260	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
6261	  % {\v C}ech vanishing on an affine open) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52).
6262	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
6263	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
6264	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
6265	  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
6266	  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
6267	  % all $p > 0$."
6268	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
6269	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}.}
6270	  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(M\) an \(R\)-module, \(f \in R\), and \(g : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to R\) a finite
6271	  family with \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Then for every \(p > 0\),
6272	  \[
6273	    \check{H}^p\bigl(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}\bigr) = 0.
6274	  \]
6275	\end{lemma}
6276	
6277	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write $U = \Spec(A)$ for some ring $A$.
6278	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
6279	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
6280	% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
6281	% Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
6282	% $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
6283	% is identified with the complex
6284	% $\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to \ldots$"
6285	\begin{proof}
6286	  \uses{lem:section_cech_module_exact, lem:section_cech_homology_exact,
6287	    lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway, lem:away_comparison_isLocalizedModule,
6288	    lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway, lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away}
6289	  Set \(R_f = \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\). Following the Stacks proof's ``Write
6290	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\)'' step, we run the whole computation over the ring \(R_f\), where the
6291	  cover \emph{does} span the unit ideal. The two algebraic inputs this needs are read off from the
6292	  geometry of the cover.
6293	
6294	  \emph{(a) Each \(g_i\) lies in the radical of \((f)\).} From \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) we have in
6295	  particular \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\) for every \(i\), and the inclusion of basic opens
6296	  \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\) is equivalent to \(g_i \in \sqrt{(f)}\) (a power of \(g_i\) is a multiple of
6297	  \(f\)). \emph{(b) The cover spans over \(R_f\).} Because \(f\) is a unit in \(R_f\), the equality
6298	  \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) pulls back to \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i/1)\), so the
6299	  images \(g_i/1\) span the unit ideal of \(R_f\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}).
6300	
6301	  With (a) and (b) in hand, Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact_of_localizationAway} supplies the
6302	  positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet(g, M)\):
6303	  internally it instantiates the polymorphic exactness of
6304	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} over \(R_f\) with the module
6305	  \(M_f = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} f)\, M\), and transports the
6306	  result back along the degreewise isomorphisms \(M_{g_\sigma} \cong (M_f)_{g_\sigma}\) of
6307	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isLocalizedModule_comp_away} (for a tuple \(\sigma\), \(g_\sigma = \prod_k g_{\sigma k}
6308	  \in \sqrt{(f)}\), so both sides present \(M\) localised away from \(g_\sigma f\)).
6309	
6310	  Finally, the tilde-bridge ladder identifies the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
6311	  \(\{D(g_i)\}\) with this localised-module complex --- via the same section-to-module identification
6312	  and coface matching used in Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} --- and wraps positive-degree
6313	  exactness as the vanishing of the degree-\(p\) homology, giving
6314	  \(\check{H}^p(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
6315	\end{proof}

6317	The {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis
6318	(Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) is proved by a dimension shift on the
6319	coefficient module, decomposed below into four sub-lemmas: the short exact sequence of
6320	{\v C}ech complexes coming from a basis (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}), the
6321	resulting stability of vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology under the injective
6322	quotient (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), the sheaf-cohomology base case
6323	\(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}), and the
6324	dimension-shift induction \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\)
6325	(Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}). The top lemma's proof then just
6326	chains these.
6327	
6328	\subsection{Section {\v C}ech infrastructure and the cover-system encoding}
6329	
6330	The sub-lemmas below are formalized in a \emph{cover-local}, presheaf-level, hypothesis-driven
6331	form: each lemma fixes a single index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) and works with the
6332	section {\v C}ech complexes of that one covering, taking the geometric inputs (section-level
6333	exactness on faces, positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing) as hypotheses. The cover-global
6334	\((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) data of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis} re-enters only
6335	in the inductive step (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}), where it is packaged by
6336	the cover-system encoding (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system},
6337	Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) and discharges those hypotheses via the per-face
6338	short-exact-sequence derivation (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}). The supporting
6339	definitions and the degreewise product engine are collected first.

6341	\begin{definition}
6342	
6343	\leanok
6344	[{\v C}ech cohomology accessor]
6345	  \label{def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
6346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechCohomology}
6347	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex}
6348	  \textit{Project-local accessor.}
6349	  For an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)), a
6350	  presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(p \geq 0\), the \emph{{\v C}ech
6351	  cohomology} \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the degree-\(p\) homology of the section
6352	  {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex},
6353	  \[
6354	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\;
6355	      H^p\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr),
6356	  \]
6357	  taken as an object of \(\mathrm{Ab}\). This is the named wrapper that the 01EO chain refers to
6358	  uniformly.
6359	\end{definition}

689	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
690	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family

1181	
1182	[Standard-cover {\v C}ech-complex vanishing on affines]
1183	  \label{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
1184	  % NOTE: The top-level Lean target vanishes the absolute section {\v C}ech

1207	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}

1209	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
1210	  % {\v C}ech vanishing) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52).
1211	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
1212	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1213	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
1214	  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
1215	  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
1216	  % all $p > 0$."
1217	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
1218	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
1219	  vanishing).}
1220	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) the associated
1221	  affine scheme, and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
1222	  (\(\mathcal{F} : (\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Modules}\)). Let
1223	  \(s : \iota \to R\) (\(\iota\) finite) be a spanning family of the unit ideal,
1224	  \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\), and let
1225	  \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in \iota} D(s_i)\) be the associated standard
1226	  affine cover of Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}; the index set and the
1227	  affine pieces \(D(s_i)\) with sections \(R_{s_i}\) are read off directly from the
1228	  spanning family. The statement is \emph{intrinsic} to \(\operatorname{Spec} R\):
1229	  there is no relative morphism \(f\) and no pushforward in the conclusion. Then the
1230	  \emph{section {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1231	  \mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- the cochain
1232	  complex of abelian groups whose degree-\(p\) term is
1233	  \(\prod_{\sigma}\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) --- has vanishing cohomology in all

6898	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let
6899	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and suppose there is a set
6900	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of open coverings such that: (1) every \(\mathcal{U} :
6901	  U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) has \(U, U_i \in \mathcal{B}\) and
6902	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\); (2) for each
6903	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
6904	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
6905	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,
6906	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Then

6914	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly

688	\noindent
689	We summarize the results obtained so far.
690	
691	\begin{lemma}
692	\label{lemma-spec-sheaves}
693	Let $R$ be a ring. Let $M$ be an $R$-module. Let $\widetilde M$
694	be the sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$-modules
695	associated to $M$.
696	\begin{enumerate}
697	\item We have $\Gamma(\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}) = R$.
698	\item We have $\Gamma(\Spec(R), \widetilde M) = M$ as an $R$-module.
699	\item For every $f \in R$ we have
700	$\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}) = R_f$.
701	\item For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
702	as an $R_f$-module.
703	\item Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
704	on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
705	are the maps
706	$R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
707	\ref{lemma-standard-open}.
708	\item Let $\mathfrak p$ be a prime of $R$, and let $x \in \Spec(R)$
709	be the corresponding point. We have
710	$\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R), x} = R_{\mathfrak p}$.
711	\item Let $\mathfrak p$ be a prime of $R$, and let $x \in \Spec(R)$
712	be the corresponding point. We have $\widetilde M_x = M_{\mathfrak p}$
713	as an $R_{\mathfrak p}$-module.
714	\end{enumerate}
715	Moreover, all these identifications are functorial in the $R$
716	module $M$. In particular, the functor $M \mapsto \widetilde M$
717	is an exact functor from the category of $R$-modules
718	to the category of $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$-modules.
719	\end{lemma}
720	
721	\begin{proof}
722	Assertions (1) - (7) are clear from the discussion above.
723	The exactness of the functor $M \mapsto \widetilde M$
724	follows from the fact that the functor $M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak p}$
725	is exact and the fact that exactness of short exact sequences
726	may be checked on stalks, see
727	Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-abelian}.
728	\end{proof}
729	
730	\begin{definition}
731	\label{definition-affine-scheme}
732	An {\it affine scheme} is a locally ringed space isomorphic

38	Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
39	of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
40	where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
41	the unit ideal, see
42	Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}.
43	
44	\begin{lemma}
45	\label{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
46	Let $X$ be a scheme.
47	Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
48	Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
49	open covering of an affine open of $X$.
50	Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
51	all $p > 0$.
52	\end{lemma}
53	
54	\begin{proof}
55	Write $U = \Spec(A)$ for some ring $A$.
56	In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
57	which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
58	Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
59	Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
60	$\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
61	is identified with the complex
62	$$
63	\prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
64	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
65	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
66	\ldots
67	$$
68	We are asked to show that the extended complex
69	\begin{equation}
70	\label{equation-extended}
71	0 \to
72	M \to
73	\prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
74	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
75	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
76	\ldots
77	\end{equation}
78	(whose truncation we have studied in
79	Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
80	It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
81	is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
82	Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
83	In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
84	at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
85	
86	\medskip\noindent
87	There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
88	Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. Note that
89	$M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$. Similarly
90	for a localization at a product $f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}$ and $\mathfrak p$
91	we can drop any $f_{i_j}$ for which $i_j = i_{\text{fix}}$.
92	Let us define a homotopy
93	$$
94	h :
95	\prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}}
96	M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
97	\longrightarrow
98	\prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
99	M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}
100	$$
101	by the rule
102	$$
103	h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}
104	$$
105	(This is ``dual'' to the homotopy in the proof of
106	Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-homology-complex}.)
107	In other words, $h : \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}, \mathfrak p} \to M_\mathfrak p$
108	is projection onto the factor
109	$M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$ and in general
110	the map $h$ equal projection onto the factors
111	$M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}} f_{i_1} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
112	= M_{f_{i_1} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}$. We compute

541	
542	/-- **Distinguished opens of `Spec R` are affine opens.**  `D(r) ⊆ Spec R` is affine, being
543	isomorphic to `Spec R[1/r]` via `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`.  Project-local: places each distinguished
544	open into the enlarged affine-open basis, discharging `faces_mem` for `affineCoverSystemGeneral`. -/
545	theorem isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (r : R) :
546	    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) :=
547	  IsAffine.of_isIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway r).hom
548	
549	/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a general affine open** (Stacks 02KG,
550	Tag 009L), generalising `standard_cover_cofinal` from a distinguished `D(f)` to an *arbitrary affine
551	open* `V` of `Spec R`.  Given an arbitrary open cover `W : α → (Spec R).Opens` of `V`, there is a
552	finite standard subcover refining it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `V = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` and each
553	`D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)`.  The only change from `standard_cover_cofinal` is the source of
554	quasi-compactness: `IsAffineOpen.isCompact` in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen`.  The
555	refinement step for `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` over a general affine open. -/
556	theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
557	    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {α : Type u} (W : α → (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
558	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
559	      V = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
560	      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
561	  classical
562	  have hK : IsCompact (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) := hV.isCompact
563	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
564	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
565	  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
566	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `V ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
567	  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ V ⊓ W p.2}
568	  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
569	  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
570	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `V`.
571	  have hsub : (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
572	    intro x hx
573	    have hxV : x ∈ V := hx
574	    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxV)
575	    have hxinf : x ∈ ((V ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
576	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
577	      exact ⟨hxV, hxa⟩
578	    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
579	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (V ⊓ W a).isOpen
580	    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
581	    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
582	    have hle : B r ≤ V ⊓ W a := by
583	      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
584	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
585	    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
586	    rw [hr']; exact hxv
587	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
588	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
589	  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.
590	  let e := t.equivFin
591	  refine ⟨t.card, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.1, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.2, ?_, ?_⟩
592	  · apply le_antisymm
593	    · intro x hx
594	      obtain ⟨i, hit, hxi⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht hx)
595	      refine TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨e ⟨i, hit⟩, ?_⟩
596	      change x ∈ B (e.symm (e ⟨i, hit⟩)).1.1.1
597	      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi
598	    · exact iSup_le fun i => le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_left
599	  · intro i
600	    exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_right
601	
602	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
603	-- The `ses_cech_h1` assembly over a general affine open is heartbeat-heavy (large product
604	-- presheaf-section equalities); the bound matches `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.
605	/-- **Section surjectivity for the enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing`
606	field, general-affine-open form), generalising `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from a distinguished `D f`
607	to an *arbitrary affine open* `V₀`.  Let `S : 0 → S₁ → S₂ → S₃ → 0` be short exact with `S₁` having
608	vanishing positive Čech cohomology over every standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union is affine*
609	(`hvanish`). Then the section map `S₂(V₀) → S₃(V₀)` is surjective over every affine open `V₀`.
610	
611	Same proof as `affine_surj_of_vanishing`, with the quasi-compact refinement supplied by
612	`standard_cover_cofinal_affine` (using `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`) and the cover's affineness
613	(`V₀ = ⨆ D(gᵢ)`) fed to `hvanish`. Project-local: discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of
614	`affineCoverSystemGeneral`. -/
615	theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
616	    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
617	    (hvanish : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
618	      IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
619	          (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
620	      ∀ (q : ℕ), 0 < q →
621	        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
622	          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁) q))
623	    (V₀ : (Spec R).Opens) (hV₀ : IsAffineOpen V₀) :
624	    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
625	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
626	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
627	          (Opposite.op V₀))) := by
628	  classical
629	  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁ with hFX
630	  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₂ with hGX
631	  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₃ with hHX
632	  set fι := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
633	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfι
634	  set gπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
635	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgπ
636	  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
637	  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
638	  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
639	    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
640	      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
641	  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gπ :=
642	    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
643	      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
644	  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
645	  intro t
646	  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
647	  have hch : ∀ p : ↥V₀, ∃ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ≤ V₀)
648	      (sl : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))),
649	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op W)) sl
650	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWle).op) t ∧ (p : Spec R) ∈ W := by
651	    intro p
652	    obtain ⟨W, hWle, ⟨sl, hsl⟩, hmem⟩ := hls V₀ t p.1 p.2
653	    exact ⟨W, hWle, sl, hsl, hmem⟩
654	  choose W hWle sLift hsLift hmem using hch
655	  have hVcov : V₀ ≤ ⨆ p : ↥V₀, W p := by
656	    intro x hx
657	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨x, hx⟩, hmem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
658	  -- Step 3: refine to a standard cover `D(gᵢ)` of the affine open `V₀`.
659	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hVeq, hgle⟩ := standard_cover_cofinal_affine V₀ hV₀ W hVcov
660	  set U : ULift.{u} (Fin n) → (Spec R).Opens := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) with hU
661	  have hUsup : ⨆ i, U i = V₀ := by
662	    rw [hU, hVeq]
663	    exact Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))
664	  -- the cover's union is affine (it equals `V₀`), so it is an admissible covering.
665	  have haff : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
666	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) := hUsup ▸ hV₀
667	  -- the restricted local lifts on the standard cover
668	  set sLoc : ∀ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i))) :=
669	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)

740	
741	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the enlarged affine cover system -/
742	
743	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
744	-- The structure literal forces three nontrivial field elaborations (`faces_mem`,
745	-- `surj_of_vanishing`, `injective_acyclic`), each unfolding large presheaf data.
746	/-- **The enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG), with basis `B` the *all affine opens* of
747	`Spec R` (rather than only the distinguished `D f` of `affineCoverSystem`) and admissible coverings
748	`Cov` the standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine.  The three fields:
749	- `faces_mem` ← faces `⨅ₖ D(g_{σk}) = D(∏ g)` are distinguished, hence affine
750	  (`isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen`);
751	- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` (general-affine-open surjectivity);
752	- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity).
753	Project-local: the enlarged instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by
754	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` to obtain general-affine-open Serre vanishing. -/
755	noncomputable def affineCoverSystemGeneral (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
756	  B := { U : (Spec R).Opens | IsAffineOpen U }
757	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
758	    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
759	    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
760	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) }
761	  faces_mem := by
762	    rintro c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ p σ
763	    have h := basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) σ
764	    exact h ▸ isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen
765	      (∏ k, (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) (σ k))
766	  surj_of_vanishing := by
767	    rintro S hSE hvanish V hV
768	    refine affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine S hSE (fun n g haff q hq => ?_) V hV
769	    exact hvanish ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩
770	      ⟨n, g, rfl, haff⟩ q hq
771	  injective_acyclic := by
772	    rintro I hI c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
773	    haveI : Injective I := hI
774	    have hfam := injective_cech_acyclicFam
775	      (U := fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) I q hq
776	    exact hfam
777	
778	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — general-affine-open Serre vanishing (reduced to the seed)
779	
780	The **residual** of the enlarged route is the positive-degree quasi-coherent Čech-vanishing *seed*
781	`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F)` for `p > 0` over
782	every standard cover `𝒰 : i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine* (not just a distinguished
783	`D(f)`). Unlike the distinguished case — discharged by
784	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` via change of base to a single
785	`Rf = Localization.Away f` — a general affine open `V = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` has no single `f` with `V = D(f)`,
786	so the seed needs a change of base to `Γ(V, 𝒪)` (equivalently, the identification of the section
787	Čech complex over `{D(gᵢ) ⊆ Spec R}` with the standard-cover complex of `~(Γ(V,F))` over
788	`Spec Γ(V)` under `V ≅ Spec Γ(V)`). That change-of-scheme identification is the lone remaining
789	geometric leaf; it is isolated here as the explicit hypothesis `hseed`. -/
790	
791	/-- **Reduction of the general-affine-open quasi-coherent seed to the tilde case.**  For a
792	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, transporting along `F ≅ ~M` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`,
793	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F` to the
794	tilde-case positive-degree Čech vanishing `htilde`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, ~M)` over every standard
795	cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union is affine.  Verbatim analogue of
796	`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` for the enlarged cover system; isolates the single
797	geometric residual (the change-of-base-to-`Γ(V)` tilde-section identification).  Project-local: the
798	cleanest statement of the general-affine-open seed residual. -/
799	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
800	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
801	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
802	        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
803	            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
804	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
805	          IsZero (cechCohomology
806	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
807	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
808	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
809	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F := by
810	  intro c hc p hp
811	  obtain ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
812	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
813	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
814	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
815	  exact htilde n g hcov p hp
816	
817	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the Čech-vanishing seed** (Stacks 02KG,
818	generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).  For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
819	and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
820	vanishes for `p > 0`, modulo the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed `hseed`.  Instantiates the
821	basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at the enlarged cover system
822	`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (whose basis is all affine opens).  Project-local: the Need #2 target
823	of `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`, isolating the single seed residual. -/
824	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
825	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
826	    (hseed : HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F)
827	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
828	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
829	  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) hseed V hV p hp e
830	
831	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the tilde Čech-vanishing leaf**
832	(Stacks 02KG, generalised).  Composes `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` (seed
833	from tilde vanishing) with `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed`: for quasi-coherent `F` and any
834	affine open `V`, `Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F) = 0` for `p > 0`, modulo the tilde-section change-of-base
835	leaf `htilde`.  Project-local: the fully-reduced Need #2 top, leaving exactly the change-of-scheme
836	tilde residual. -/
837	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
838	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
839	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
840	        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
841	            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
842	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
843	          IsZero (cechCohomology
844	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
845	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
846	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p))
847	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
848	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
849	  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed F
850	    (affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing F htilde) V hV p hp e
851	
852	end AlgebraicGeometry

## standard_cover_cofinal (line ~144) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement (built):**
  ```
  theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {α : Type u}
      (W : α → (Spec R).Opens)
      (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
      ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
        ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i)
  ```
- **Approach:** quasi-compactness of `D(f)` (`PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen`) + basic-open basis
  (`PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens` +
  `TopologicalSpace.IsTopologicalBasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open`) +
  `IsCompact.elim_finite_subcover`. Index type `I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ Uf ⊓ W p.2}` carries the
  witness `a`; finite `Finset I` repackaged to `Fin t.card` via `t.equivFin`.
- **Key technique (reuse downstream):** the `(Spec R).Opens` (carrier `↥(Spec R)`) vs
  `PrimeSpectrum R` defeq impedance is tamed by (a) `set Uf : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f`,
  and (b) a local `let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r`. Coercing a
  *variable* of type `(Spec R).Opens` to `Set ↥(Spec R)` works; coercing an inline
  `(PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens)` ascription does NOT (it fails to insert the SetLike
  coercion). Bind to `Uf`/`B` first. Use `SetLike.coe_subset_coe`, `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup`,
  `TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf`, `Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp`, `Equiv.symm_apply_apply`.
- **Note for surj consumer:** statement is shaped to feed `ses_cech_h1` directly — `⨆ D(gᵢ) = D(f)`
  (so `iSup U = D(f)`, matching `s : H(op (iSup U))`) and `D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)` (so local lifts on
  `W (φ i)` restrict to `D(gᵢ)`). `φ` and the `≤` are both essential for the lift-transport.

1347	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1348	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1349	
1350	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1351	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1352	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1353	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1354	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1355	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1356	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1357	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1358	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1359	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1360	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1361	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1362	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1363	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1364	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1365	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1366	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1367	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1368	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1369	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1370	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
1371	
1372	\begin{definition}
1373	
1374	[Free presheaf complex of a cover]
1375	  \label{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1376	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex,
1377	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYoneda,
1378	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverOpen,
1379	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen,

7060	  \textit{Project-local.}
7061	  The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is the value of the
7062	  nerve at the initial augmentation object, namely \((\mathrm{id}_X)_* (\mathrm{id}_X)^* \mathcal{F}\).
7063	  The \emph{augmentation point isomorphism} is the canonical isomorphism
7064	  \[
7065	    (\mathrm{CechNerve}\ \mathcal{U}\ \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{left} \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}
7066	  \]
7067	  obtained by composing the pullback unitor \((\mathrm{id}_X)^* \mathcal{F} \cong \mathcal{F}\) with the
7068	  pushforward unitor \((\mathrm{id}_X)_* \mathcal{G} \cong \mathcal{G}\) of the push--pull adjunction.
7069	\end{definition}
7070	
7071	\begin{definition}
7072	
7073	\leanok
7074	[{\v C}ech augmentation map]
7075	  \label{def:cech_augmentation}
7076	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentation}
7077	  \uses{def:cech_nerve_point_iso, def:cech_complex_on_X}
7078	  \textit{Project-local.}
7079	  The \emph{{\v C}ech augmentation} is the morphism \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\) of
7080	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules obtained by composing the inverse of the augmentation point
7081	  isomorphism of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve_point_iso} with the degree-\(0\) component of the
7082	  nerve's augmentation natural transformation (which lands in the degree-\(0\) term \(\mathcal{C}^0\) of
7083	  the complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex_on_X}).
7084	\end{definition}
7085	
7086	\begin{lemma}
7087	[The augmentation kills the first differential]
7088	  \label{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
7089	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentation_comp_d,
7090	    AlgebraicGeometry.augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero}
7091	  \uses{def:cech_augmentation}
7092	  \textit{Project-local.}
7093	  The composite of the augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\) of
7094	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation} with the first {\v C}ech differential vanishes,
7095	  \[
7096	    \varepsilon \cdot d^0 \;=\; 0.
7097	  \]
7098	\end{lemma}
7099	\begin{proof}
7100	  \uses{def:cech_augmentation}
7101	  This is the standard augmented-cosimplicial identity. The differential \(d^0 = \delta^0 - \delta^1\)
7102	  is the alternating sum of the two cofaces, and naturality of the augmentation natural transformation
7103	  of the cosimplicial object intertwines \(\varepsilon\) with \(\delta^0\) and with \(\delta^1\) in the
7104	  same way; the two contributions therefore cancel.
7105	\end{proof}
7106	
7107	\begin{definition}
7108	
7109	\leanok
7110	[Augmented {\v C}ech complex]
7111	  \label{def:cech_augmented_complex}
7112	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentedComplex}
7113	  \uses{def:cech_complex_on_X, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
7114	  \textit{Project-local.}
7115	  The \emph{augmented {\v C}ech complex} is the cochain complex
7116	  \[
7117	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots
7118	  \]
7119	  in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) obtained by prepending the coefficient sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) in degree \(0\)
7120	  to the complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex_on_X} along the augmentation
7121	  \(\varepsilon\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}, using the augmentation identity
7122	  \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}. Concretely its degree-\(0\)
7123	  term is \(\mathcal{F}\) and its degree-\((p+1)\) term is \(\mathcal{C}^p\). This is the object whose
7124	  exactness is the content of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}.
7125	\end{definition}
7126	
7127	\begin{lemma}
7128	[Sheafification kills a presheaf that is locally bijective to a locally-zero one]
7129	  \label{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}
7130	  \lean{CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W,
7131	    CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero,
7132	    CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective}
7133	  \textit{Project-bespoke site-theory lemma (pure Mathlib site theory).}
7134	  Let \(J\) be a Grothendieck topology on a site \(\mathcal{C}\) and let \(J.W\) denote the
7135	  class of \emph{locally bijective} maps of presheaves --- the local-isomorphism class for
7136	  \(J\), i.e.\ the maps inverted by sheafification. Then:
7137	  \begin{enumerate}
7138	    \item Sheafification \(\operatorname{presheafToSheaf}\) carries every map in \(J.W\) to an
7139	      isomorphism; consequently it sends a presheaf that is locally bijective to a presheaf
7140	      with zero sheafification to a presheaf with zero sheafification. Precisely, if
7141	      \(\varphi : P \to Q\) lies in \(J.W\) and the sheafification of \(Q\) is the zero
7142	      sheaf, then the sheafification of \(P\) is the zero sheaf.
7143	    \item In particular, if \(P\) admits a locally bijective map to the zero presheaf --- for
7144	      instance if \(P\) is locally zero, i.e.\ vanishes on a basis of the topology --- then
7145	      the sheafification of \(P\) is the zero sheaf.
7146	    \item For an abelian-valued presheaf the locally-bijective hypothesis is the conjunction
7147	      of local injectivity and local surjectivity of the comparison map; a map that is both
7148	      locally injective and locally surjective lies in \(J.W\).
7149	  \end{enumerate}
7150	\end{lemma}
7151	\begin{proof}
7152	  Sheafification \(\operatorname{presheafToSheaf} : \widehat{\mathcal{C}} \to
7153	  \operatorname{Sh}(J)\) inverts exactly the maps of the class \(J.W\): by definition,
7154	  \(\varphi \in J.W\) if and only if \(\operatorname{presheafToSheaf}(\varphi)\) is an
7155	  isomorphism. Hence for \(\varphi : P \to Q\) in \(J.W\) the induced map
7156	  \(\operatorname{sheafify}(P) \to \operatorname{sheafify}(Q)\) is an isomorphism, and
7157	  being a zero object transports along an isomorphism: if \(\operatorname{sheafify}(Q)\)
7158	  is zero then so is \(\operatorname{sheafify}(P)\). This proves (1); part (2) is the
7159	  special case \(Q = 0\) (the zero presheaf, whose sheafification is the zero sheaf),
7160	  and a presheaf that vanishes on a basis admits such a locally bijective map to \(0\).
7161	  For (3), sheafification is additive, and a map of abelian presheaves that is
7162	  simultaneously locally injective and locally surjective is a local isomorphism,
7163	  i.e.\ lies in \(J.W\); applying (1) then collapses its sheafification.
7164	\end{proof}
7165	
7166	\begin{lemma}
7167	
7168	[A faithful zero-preserving functor reflects zero objects]
7169	  \label{lem:isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms}
7170	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms,
7171	    CategoryTheory.Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms}
7172	  % NOTE: CategoryTheory.Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms is an
7173	  % import-isolation duplicate of the AlgebraicGeometry version, carrying the identical
7174	  % statement and proof; it lives in OpenImmersionPushforward.lean because that file is not in
7175	  % the import chain of the file holding the original. Both \lean{} targets resolve to this one
7176	  % block.
7177	  Let \(F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a functor between categories with zero
7178	  morphisms that is \emph{faithful} and \emph{preserves zero morphisms}. If the image
7179	  \(F(Y)\) of an object \(Y\) is a zero object of \(\mathcal{D}\), then \(Y\) is a zero
7180	  object of \(\mathcal{C}\).
7181	\end{lemma}
7182	\begin{proof}
7183	
7184	  An object \(Y\) is a zero object if and only if its identity morphism equals its zero
7185	  endomorphism, \(\mathrm{id}_Y = 0\). Applying this characterization to \(F(Y)\), the
7186	  hypothesis gives \(\mathrm{id}_{F(Y)} = 0\). Since \(F\) preserves identities and zero
7187	  morphisms, \(F(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{F(Y)} = 0 = F(0)\); faithfulness of \(F\)
7188	  then makes the map on hom-sets injective, so \(\mathrm{id}_Y = 0\), whence \(Y\) is a
7189	  zero object.
7190	\end{proof}
7191	
7192	\begin{lemma}
7193	
7194	\leanok
7195	[Sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes]
7196	  \label{lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}
7197	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}
7198	  \uses{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}
7199	  Let \(J\) be a Grothendieck topology on a site \(\mathcal{C}\) and let \(Q\) be a
7200	  presheaf of abelian groups on \(\mathcal{C}\). Suppose that for every object \(U\) there
7201	  is a covering sieve \(S \in J(U)\) all of whose members \(g : V \to U\) land on an object
7202	  \(V\) where \(Q(V)\) is a zero object. Then the sheafification of \(Q\) is the zero
7203	  sheaf.
7204	\end{lemma}
7205	\begin{proof}
7206	  \uses{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}
7207	  This is the ``locally zero'' packaging of
7208	  Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}. Let \(Z\) be the constant presheaf with
7209	  value the zero group; its sheafification is the zero sheaf. The zero map \(0 : Q \to Z\)
7210	  is locally injective --- on each covering sieve furnished by the hypothesis the source
7211	  sections \(Q(V)\) are subsingletons, so the comparison is injective locally --- and it
7212	  is locally surjective because the target is objectwise a zero object. Hence
7213	  \(0 : Q \to Z\) is locally bijective, i.e.\ lies in the local-isomorphism class
7214	  \(J.W\), and part (3) followed by part (1) of
7215	  Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero} collapses the sheafification of \(Q\) to the
7216	  zero sheaf.
7217	\end{proof}
7218	
7219	\begin{lemma}
7220	
7221	\leanok
7222	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
7223	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
7224	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
7225	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:cech_augmented_complex, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy,
7226	    lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec, lem:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology,
7227	    lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero, lem:isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms,
7228	    lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero, lem:cechSection_isZero_homology}
7229	  % NOTE: the augmented-complex object layer (cechComplexOnX, cechNervePointIso, cechAugmentation,
7230	  % cechAugmentation_comp_d, cechAugmentedComplex) is formalized in CechHigherDirectImage.lean.
7231	  % The \lean{} target cechAugmented_exact (the exactness theorem) is now PLACED in the new
7232	  % downstream file CechAugmentedResolution.lean — NOT in CechHigherDirectImage.lean. Reason
7233	  % (file placement, NOT a math gap): cechAugmented_exact CANNOT be proved in
7234	  % CechHigherDirectImage.lean because every ingredient of the sections/sheafification route

\label{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_complex_iso}
  % NOTE: (iter-056 prover + lvb-csi) MIS-SPECIFIED — statement is FALSE as written and
  % internally inconsistent with def:cech_augmented_complex. The augmented complex D has
  % D^0 = Γ(V,F), not Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_0); the non-augmented D' has D'^0 = ∏_i Γ(U_i∩V,F).
  % So D ≅ D' fails (they differ at degree 0 by the sheaf property). The correct target is
  % D ≅ D'_aug := (sectionCechComplex …).augment ε hε, where ε : Γ(V,F) → ∏_i Γ(U_i∩V,F) is the
  % restriction product and hε : ε ≫ d⁰ = 0. Blueprint-writer must re-state before re-dispatch.
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmented_complex}
  Fix an open \(V \subseteq X\). The augmented {\v C}ech complex of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} evaluated at \(V\) (through
  \(\operatorname{toPresheaf}\)) --- call it \(D^\bullet\), the cochain complex of abelian
  groups with \(D^p = \Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) --- is
  isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to the concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet\)
  over the family \(\bigl(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\cdot \cap V\bigr)\): the
  complex with \(D'^p = \prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) and the
  alternating coface restriction differential. The isomorphism is the degreewise map of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}, and it intertwines the two differentials.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
  In each degree \(p\) the object isomorphism \(D^p \cong D'^p\) is
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}. It remains to check the degreewise isomorphisms
  commute with the differentials. The differential of \(D'^\bullet\) is, read through the
  product equivalence \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), the alternating sum of presheaf face
  restrictions \(\sum_i (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) computed in
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}. The differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the
  evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech-nerve coface maps, assembled from the same push--pull
  restriction comparisons; under the degreewise identification each coface of \(D^\bullet\)
  becomes the corresponding face restriction of \(D'^\bullet\), so the alternating sums
  agree. Reusing the alternating-sum bookkeeping of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
  --- rather than rebuilding it --- matches the differentials and yields the isomorphism of
  complexes.
\end{proof}
input.new_string:
\label{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_complex_iso}
  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The iso is to the AUGMENTED concrete section
  % complex D'_aug := (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε hε, NOT to the bare section complex.
  % The evaluated augmented complex D carries Γ(V,F) at its augmentation node (def:cech_augmented_complex
  % prepends F there), which the bare section complex does not have; the two agree only after the
  % section complex is augmented along ε. The prover re-signs the Lean declaration to this augmented
  % target.
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmented_complex,
    def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  Fix an open \(V \subseteq X\). The augmented {\v C}ech complex of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} evaluated at \(V\) (through
  \(\operatorname{toPresheaf}\)) --- call it \(D^\bullet\), the cochain complex of abelian
  groups whose augmentation node is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) and whose {\v C}ech degree-\(p\)
  term is \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) --- is isomorphic, as a
  cochain complex, to the \emph{augmented} concrete section {\v C}ech complex
  \[
    D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}} \;:=\;
    \bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F})\bigr).\operatorname{augment}
      \varepsilon\, h_\varepsilon
  \]
  built from the concrete section {\v C}ech complex over the restricted family
  \(U'_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V\) (degree-\(p\) term
  \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma \cap V, \mathcal{F})\), alternating coface restriction
  differential) by prepending the augmentation node \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the restriction product map \(\varepsilon : \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F}) \to
      \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\), \(t \mapsto \bigl(t|_{U_i \cap V}\bigr)_i\)
      --- the evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation
      \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}; and
    \item the augmentation identity \(h_\varepsilon : \varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\), the
      evaluation at \(V\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}.
  \end{itemize}
  Concretely: at the augmentation node both complexes carry \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\),
  identified by the identity map; at {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism
  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p) \cong \prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}; and these degreewise maps
  intertwine the two differentials, including the augmentation differential \(\varepsilon\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  The augmentation node of both complexes is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), matched by the
  identity. At {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism between
  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) and \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}. It remains to check the
  degreewise isomorphisms commute with the differentials.

  \emph{{\v C}ech differentials (degrees \(p \geq 0\)).} The differential of the section
  complex is, read through the product equivalence \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), the alternating sum of presheaf face
  restrictions \(\sum_i (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) computed in
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}. The differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the
  evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech-nerve coface maps, assembled from the same push--pull
  restriction comparisons; under the degreewise identification each coface of \(D^\bullet\)
  becomes the corresponding face restriction, so the alternating sums agree. Reusing the
  alternating-sum bookkeeping of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} --- rather than
  rebuilding it --- matches these differentials.

  \emph{Augmentation differential.} The lowest differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the evaluation
  at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\)
  (Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}); read through the degree-\(0\) instance of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso} it is exactly the restriction product map
  \(t \mapsto (t|_{U_i \cap V})_i\), which is the augmentation map \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) by construction. Both augmented complexes are well-formed by the
  shared identity \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}).
  Hence the degreewise maps assemble to an isomorphism of cochain complexes
  \(D^\bullet \cong D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).
\end{proof}

\label{lem:cechSection_contractible}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_contractible}
  % NOTE: (iter-056 prover + lvb-csi) PROVABLY FALSE as written — the NON-augmented section Čech
  % complex D' is NOT contractible. Counterexample: a one-member cover {V} gives D'^0 = Fp(V),
  % d⁰ = δ₀−δ₁ = 0, so H⁰(D') = Fp(V) ≠ 0. Only the AUGMENTED complex D'_aug = D'.augment ε hε is
  % contractible. Correct target = Homotopy (𝟙 D'_aug) 0. Also: the depHomotopy engine covers ONLY
  % degrees ≥1; the augmentation node (degree 0, ε split mono) needs a separate sheaf-equalizer
  % argument — the current proof sketch is silent on it. Blueprint-writer must re-state + extend.
  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex,
    def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
  Suppose \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) for some fixed index
  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), i.e.\ \(V\) lies inside one cover member. Then the concrete section
  {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} over the family
  \(U'_j = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,j \cap V\) admits a contracting homotopy
  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet} \simeq 0\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex}
  Because \(V \le U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the restricted family \(U'_j = U_j \cap V\) has a
  \emph{maximum}: \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V = V\), and every
  \(U'_j \le V\). Consequently, for each multi-index \(\sigma\),
  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap U_\sigma \cap V = U_\sigma \cap
  V = U'_\sigma\): prepending the index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) does not change the open, so the
  prepend map is the \emph{identity} on each coefficient \(\Gamma(U'_\sigma, \mathcal{F})\)
  (the membership criterion \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex}). The dependent-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech
  engine --- \(\operatorname{depDiff}\), \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\),
  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy\_spec}\), \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (here Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is cited only as
  the Lean home of the \(\operatorname{dep\ast}\) engine declarations --- its standard-cover {\v
  C}ech-vanishing \emph{conclusion} is \emph{not} a mathematical dependency of the present
  contractibility argument) --- then supplies the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
  contracting homotopy \(h\) with \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\), since its unit,
  shift, and coface-commutation compatibilities all hold with the prepend map acting as the
  identity. Packaging \(h\) as a homotopy of cochain complexes gives \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet}
  \simeq 0\).
\end{proof}
input.new_string:
\label{lem:cechSection_contractible}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_contractible}
  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The contractible complex is the AUGMENTED section
  % complex D'_aug = (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε hε, NOT the bare section complex D'
  % (which is not contractible: a one-member cover {V} gives H⁰(D') = Γ(V,F) ≠ 0). The prover
  % re-signs the Lean declaration to Homotopy (𝟙 D'_aug) 0. The dep* engine supplies only the
  % positive-degree homotopy; the augmentation node is a separate explicit degree-0 computation,
  % given below.
  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex,
    def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the projection homotopy
  % $h(s)_{i_0\ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0\ldots i_p}$ contracting the extended
  % {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L83--112).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
  % at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
  % There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
  % Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
  % Let us define a homotopy
  % $h : \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
  % \longrightarrow \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}$
  % by the rule $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
  % In other words, $h : \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}, \mathfrak p} \to M_\mathfrak p$
  % is projection onto the factor
  % $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$ ..."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof, the projection homotopy).}
  Suppose \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) for some fixed index
  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), i.e.\ \(V\) lies inside one cover member. Then the \emph{augmented}
  concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} --- the section {\v C}ech complex over the family
  \(U'_j = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,j \cap V\), augmented at
  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along the restriction product \(\varepsilon\) --- admits a
  contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex, def:cech_augmentation,
    lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  Because \(V \le U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the restricted family \(U'_j = U_j \cap V\) has a
  \emph{maximum}: \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V = V\), and every
  \(U'_j \le V\). Consequently, for each multi-index \(\sigma\),
  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap U_\sigma \cap V = U_\sigma \cap
  V = U'_\sigma\): prepending the index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) does not change the open, so the
  prepend map is the \emph{identity} on each coefficient \(\Gamma(U'_\sigma, \mathcal{F})\)
  (the membership criterion \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex}). The contracting homotopy is the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
  map, \(h(s)_\sigma = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma}\), exactly the projection homotopy of the
  Stacks proof; it splits into two parts.

  \emph{Positive {\v C}ech degrees \((p \ge 1\)).} On the section {\v C}ech part the
  dependent-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine --- \(\operatorname{depDiff}\),
  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\), \(\operatorname{depHomotopy\_spec}\),
  \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (here cited only as the
  Lean home of the \(\operatorname{dep\ast}\) engine declarations --- its standard-cover {\v
  C}ech-vanishing \emph{conclusion} is \emph{not} a mathematical dependency of the present
  argument) --- supplies the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy \(h\) with
  \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\), since its unit, shift, and coface-commutation
  compatibilities all hold with the prepend map acting as the identity on each coefficient.

  \emph{Augmentation node (the piece the engine does not supply).} The engine governs only the
  {\v C}ech degrees; the contracting-homotopy identity at the augmentation node must be checked
  directly. The relevant homotopy component runs from the lowest {\v C}ech term
  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) down to the augmentation term
  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), and is the \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-th coordinate projection
  \(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} : (s_j)_j \mapsto s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\) --- well defined because
  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = V\), so \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \Gamma(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V,
  \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\). We verify
  \(\varepsilon \circ \pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} + (\text{degree-}0\ \text{engine term}) = \mathrm{id}\)
  on \(s \in \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\). The augmentation differential
  \(\varepsilon\) is the restriction product, so
  \(\bigl(\varepsilon(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} s)\bigr)_j = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\); the
  degree-\(0\) contribution of the {\v C}ech homotopy supplies the complementary term
  \(s_j - s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\) coming from the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) shift on
  the {\v C}ech part. Their sum is \(s_j\), i.e.\ the identity. (This is the bottom node of the
  Stacks projection homotopy: \(h\) on \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to M\) is projection onto the
  factor \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}} = M\).) Together the two parts give a homotopy
  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\) of cochain complexes.
\end{proof}

By Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} the evaluated complex \(D^\bullet\) is
  isomorphic to the concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet\). The hypothesis \(V \le
  \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i\) places \(V\) inside one cover member, so
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_contractible} gives a contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet}
  \simeq 0\); transporting it across the complex isomorphism yields \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet}
  \simeq 0\). Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero} then makes
  \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) a zero object in every degree.
input.new_string:
By Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} the evaluated complex \(D^\bullet\) is
  isomorphic to the augmented concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).
  The hypothesis \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i\) places \(V\) inside one cover
  member, so Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_contractible} gives a contracting homotopy
  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\); transporting it across the complex
  isomorphism yields \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet} \simeq 0\).
  Lemma~\ref{lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero} then makes
  \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) a zero object in every degree.

8397	[Serre vanishing for a general affine open (Need~\#2)]
8398	  \label{lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open}
8399	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_serre_vanishing_general_open_TODO}
8400	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet; the name is a TODO placeholder

8403	  \uses{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis, lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing,
8404	    lem:standard_cover_cofinal, def:affine_cover_system, lem:affine_faces_mem,
8405	    lem:injective_cech_acyclic, def:absolute_cohomology}
8406	  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(V\) \emph{any} affine open of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), and
8407	  \(\mathcal{H}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module. Then for every
8408	  \(q \geq 1\) the absolute cohomology vanishes:
8409	  \[
8410	    H^q(V, \mathcal{H}) \;=\; \operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_V, \mathcal{H}) \;=\; 0.
8411	  \]
8412	  This is the general-affine-open companion of the distinguished-open Serre vanishing
8413	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): the basis \(\mathcal{B}\) of the affine cover system is
8414	  enlarged from the distinguished opens \(D(f)\) to all affine opens, while the admissible coverings
8415	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) remain the standard covers \(i \mapsto D(g_i)\).
8416	\end{lemma}

8419	    lem:standard_cover_cofinal, def:affine_cover_system, lem:affine_faces_mem,

8422	  affine cover system (Definition~\ref{def:affine_cover_system}) with its basis \(\mathcal{B}\)
8423	  enlarged to \emph{all} affine opens of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\); the admissible coverings
